accepted.question_text: Does there exist a universal constant C such that for every n and every collection of n self-adjoint real n x n matrices A_1,...,A_n with ||A_i|| <= 1, one can choose signs eps_i in {+1,-1} so that ||sum_i eps_i A_i|| <= C sqrt(n)?
accepted.context_brief: This is the Matrix Spencer discrepancy question for non-commutative matrices.

evidence.quote: Conjecture 1 (Matrix Spencer). There exists a positive universal constant C such that ... min_{epsilon in {+/-1}} || sum epsilon_i A_i || <= C sqrt(n).

accepted.question_text: For every finite group G of size n, does the Matrix Spencer bound hold for the n x n regular-representation matrices of G?
accepted.context_brief: This is the Group Spencer specialization of Matrix Spencer.

evidence.quote: Conjecture 2 (Group Spencer). Let G be a finite group of size n. Conjecture 1 holds in the particular case in which A1, ..., An are the n x n matrices corresponding to the regular representation of G.

accepted.question_text: Is a uniformly random 3-regular graph globally synchronizing with probability tending to 1 as n goes to infinity?
accepted.context_brief: Global synchronization refers to all local minima of the Kuramoto energy being global synchronized states.

evidence.quote: Conjecture 3 (Globally Synchronizing Regular Graphs). A uniform random 3-regular graph is globally synchronizing with high probability (probability going to 1 as n -> infinity).

accepted.question_text: For the random signed complete-graph matrix with off-diagonal entries +1 with probability 1/2+delta and -1 with probability 1/2-delta, and delta >= (1+epsilon) sqrt((log n)/(2n)), is the model globally synchronizing with high probability?
accepted.context_brief: This asks whether global synchrony persists under a near-critical biased random signed edge model.

evidence.quote: Conjecture 4 (Global Synchrony with negative edges)... with delta >= (1 + epsilon) sqrt(log n / 2n), is globally synchronizing with high probability.

accepted.question_text: For every epsilon > 0, does there exist a graph on n vertices with minimum degree at least (3/4 - epsilon) n that is not globally synchronizing?
accepted.context_brief: This is the extremal minimum-degree threshold problem for global synchronization.

evidence.quote: Conjecture 5 (Density threshold for Global Synchrony). For any epsilon > 0 ... minimum degree ... at least (3/4 - epsilon) n and G is not globally synchronizing.

accepted.question_text: Let x_1,...,x_n be i.i.d. N(0, I_d/d). Is there a sharp ellipsoid-fitting threshold at n/d^2 = 1/4 in the sense that for any epsilon > 0: if limsup n/d^2 <= (1-epsilon)/4 then an origin-centered ellipsoid fitting all points exists with probability -> 1, while if liminf n/d^2 >= (1+epsilon)/4 then such an ellipsoid exists with probability -> 0?
accepted.context_brief: This asks for a precise satisfiability threshold for exact centered ellipsoid fitting of random points.

evidence.quote: Conjecture 6 (Ellipsoid fitting)... lim sup n/d^2 <= (1-epsilon)/4 => P[exists ellipsoid fit]=1, lim inf n/d^2 >= (1+epsilon)/4 => P[exists ellipsoid fit]=0.

accepted.question_text: In multi-frequency synchronization over SO(2) or a finite group with uniformly random group-valued signal, what growth rate L = L_n of the number of frequency channels guarantees a polynomial-time detector that succeeds for all lambda > lambda_comp,L with lambda_comp,L < 1?
accepted.context_brief: This asks when additional frequency channels reduce the computational detection threshold below the single-frequency spectral threshold.

evidence.quote: Open Problem 7... Find a scaling L = L_n of number of frequencies such that there exists a polynomial-time algorithm ... for some lambda_comp,L < 1.

accepted.question_text: In the same synchronization setting with fixed (n-independent) number of frequencies L and lambda in the computationally hard interval (lambda_stat, 1), does there exist a sub-exponential-time detector (runtime exp(n^delta) for some delta < 1) that succeeds as n -> infinity?
accepted.context_brief: This asks for intermediate-time algorithms between polynomial and full exponential in the hard regime.

evidence.quote: Open Problem 8... when lambda in (lambda_stat, 1), does there exist a sub-exponential algorithm ... exp n^delta for some delta < 1 ...?

accepted.question_text: For the Kikuchi matrix hierarchy in symmetric Tensor PCA (even r, fixed r and ell, n -> infinity), does the eigenvalue pop-out threshold lambda_natural_{r,ell} satisfy n^(r/4) * lambda_natural_{r,ell} -> 0 as ell -> infinity (after taking n -> infinity)?
accepted.context_brief: This asks whether increasing Kikuchi hierarchy depth while keeping polynomial-time complexity can drive the normalized threshold to zero.

evidence.quote: Conjecture 9 (Kikuchi Spectral Threshold)... For fixed r, we have n^(r/4) lambda^natural_{r,ell} -> 0 as ell -> infinity (after one has taken n -> infinity).

accepted.question_text: Is there a universal constant K such that every square matrix with unit l2-norm columns has discrepancy at most K?
accepted.context_brief: This is the Komlos discrepancy conjecture in column-normalized form.

evidence.quote: Conjecture 10 (Komlos Conjecture). There exists a universal constant K such that, for all square matrices A whose columns have unit l2 norm, disc(A) <= K.

accepted.question_text: What is the exact value of sup_{n in N} sup_{A in {+/-1}^{n x n}} (1/sqrt(n)) * disc(A)?
accepted.context_brief: This asks for the best asymptotic normalized discrepancy constant in the +/-1 square-matrix setting.

evidence.quote: Open Problem 11 (How many deviations are needed afterall?). What is the value of sup_n sup_{A in {+/-1}^{n x n}} (1/sqrt(n)) disc(A).

accepted.question_text: Is limsup_{n -> infinity} sup_{A in {+/-1}^{n x n}} (1/sqrt(n)) * disc(A) strictly greater than 1?
accepted.context_brief: This asks whether the normalized discrepancy can be bounded away from 1 along an infinite family.

evidence.quote: Conjecture 12... lim sup_{n->infinity} sup_{A in {+/-1}^{n x n}} (1/sqrt(n)) disc(A) > 1.

accepted.question_text: For Sylvester Hadamard matrices H_k of order 2^k with odd k, is disc(H_k) exactly sqrt(2) * sqrt(2^k)?
accepted.context_brief: This is the unresolved odd-k discrepancy value for the Sylvester Hadamard family.

evidence.quote: Conjecture 13. Let Hk be the 2^k x 2^k Hadamard matrix obtained by the Sylvester construction... For odd k, we have disc(Hk) = sqrt(2) sqrt(2^k).

accepted.question_text: For every positive integer n divisible by 4, does there exist an n x n Hadamard matrix?
accepted.context_brief: This is the classical Hadamard existence conjecture.

evidence.quote: Conjecture 14 (Hadamard Conjecture). For any positive n a multiple of 4, there exists an n x n Hadamard matrix.

accepted.question_text: In the Sherrington-Kirkpatrick Ising model with arbitrary external field, does Glauber dynamics mix in polynomial time all the way up to the replica-symmetry-breaking threshold beta* = 1?
accepted.context_brief: This asks whether fast mixing extends to the full replica-symmetric phase boundary.

evidence.quote: Open Problem 15. Consider the SK-model with an arbitrary external field. Does Glauber Dynamics fast mix (meaning in polynomial time) up to the replica symmetry breaking threshold beta* = 1?

trace.notes: Mapped 7 entry sections plus updates and identified an explicit list of 15 open problems/conjectures in the paper.
No near-duplicate questions required merging; retained 15 distinct items.
All accepted items include page-anchored evidence and were rewritten to be self-contained without unresolved in-text references.
Saved run output to .sisyphus/paper-question-parser/runs/20260312-225527-2504.20539.json and mirrored to .sisyphus/paper-question-parser/latest.json.
